AA.note: Often associated with faster caffeine metabolism in many populations; not medical advice.

CC.note: Often associated with slower caffeine clearance; limit caffeine if sensitive (discuss with a clinician).

GG.note: Common pattern linked to continued lactase activity in many European-ancestry groups (not diagnostic).

AG.note: Intermediate lactase-related pattern; individual tolerance varies.

GA.note: Intermediate lactase-related pattern; individual tolerance varies.

AA.note: Common pattern linked to reduced lactase persistence in many populations—still not a diagnosis of intolerance.
Variant sometimes discussed with folate metabolism; supplements and diet should follow your clinician.

AG.note: Heterozygous common variant; clinical significance is person-specific.

GA.note: Heterozygous common variant; clinical significance is person-specific.

GG.note: Common reference pattern in many datasets.
